mainEntity.name: How long do I have to file a back injury claim in Salt Lake City or Boise?

acceptedAnswer.text: In Utah and Idaho, you generally have one year from the accident date to file a formal claim, but you must report the injury to your employer much sooner (usually 30-60 days). Deadlines vary by state and the type of benefit sought.
acceptedAnswer.description:
In Utah, the statute of limitations to file a formal claim with the Labor Commission is generally one year from the date of injury or the last payment of benefits, though medical-only claims may have longer windows. In Idaho, you typically have one year from the date of the accident or the last compensation payment. However, the 'notice' requirement is much shorter—usually 30 to 60 days to tell your boss. Missing these deadlines is the most common reason legitimate claims are thrown out.

Key factors include the date of the accident, when you first noticed symptoms, and when you officially notified your employer.

Check your calendar and verify when you first reported the pain. If you are approaching the one-year mark, you must file a formal application for a hearing immediately.

Missing a deadline is a fatal error; if you are unsure of your filing status, consult a lawyer to protect your rights.
